commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=6f10cce70eb57b1d9c7eb769747545971f3a43a3)
commands.review_summary:
The PR makes doctor workspace status async, reads plugin install records, calls the existing plugin-version drift detector, emits a doctor warning, awaits the caller, and adds mocked drift/error tests.

PR surface: Source +31, Tests +91. Total +122 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction: current main doctor never calls the drift detector, while gateway status already gathers and prints plugin drift. I did not establish a live stale-plugin environment in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining action is maintainer/contributor review for the daemon-aware diagnostic shape plus positive real stale-plugin proof, not a safe automated repair while contributor proof is insufficient.

target.title: fix(doctor): report official managed plugin version drift
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem**: `openclaw gateway status --deep` correctly reports plugin version drift for official managed plugins (e.g. codex remaining on 2026.5.30-beta.1 after core upgrade to 2026.6.1), but `openclaw doctor` only shows plugin counts (Loaded, Imported, Disabled, Errors) and never surfaces the drift. Operators who rely on `doctor` after upgrades miss stale runtime-critical plugins. Issue #90891.
- **Root Cause**: `noteWorkspaceStatus()` in `doctor-workspace-status.ts` calls `buildPluginRegistrySnapshotReport()` for counts but never calls `detectPluginVersionDrift()` вЂ” the existing drift detector that `gateway status --deep` already uses.
- **Fix**: Call `detectPluginVersionDrift()` after the plugin counts in `noteWorkspaceStatus()`. When drift is found, display a `Plugin version drift` warning note with the drifted plugins, installed vs expected versions, and the fix command.
- **What changed**:
  - `src/commands/doctor-workspace-status.ts`: Added drift detection call after plugin counts. Made function async. Added import for `detectPluginVersionDrift`, `loadInstalledPluginIndexInstallRecords`, and `VERSION`.
  - `src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts`: Added `await` to `noteWorkspaceStatus()` call.
  - `src/commands/doctor-workspace-status.test.ts`: Added mocks for the new imports, updated call to `await`, added test verifying drift warning includes plugin id, versions, and fix command.
- **What did NOT change**: `detectPluginVersionDrift()` itself is unchanged. `gateway status --deep` path is unchanged. The plugin counts display is unchanged.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: `openclaw doctor --non-interactive` previously showed only plugin counts with no version drift warning. After fix, it includes drift detection for official managed plugins.
- **Real environment tested**: Windows 11 Home China x64 (10.0.22631), Node 24.16.0, OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (f0df7a9). The full `openclaw doctor --non-interactive` command was run from the source checkout via `corepack pnpm openclaw doctor --non-interactive`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `corepack pnpm openclaw doctor --non-interactive`
- **Evidence after fix**: Real doctor output showing the Plugins section:

```
в—‡  Plugins в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв•®
в”‚                в”‚
в”‚  Loaded: 72    в”‚
в”‚  Imported: 0   в”‚
в”‚  Disabled: 60  в”‚
в”‚  Errors: 0     в”‚
в”‚                в”‚
в”њв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв•Ї
```

Doctor ran successfully without errors. No plugin version drift warning shown (correct вЂ” this dev environment has no stale managed plugins). The drift detection code path runs and correctly returns zero drifts.

- **Observed result after fix**: In a healthy environment with aligned plugin versions, no false warning. When drift exists (the reporter's scenario вЂ” codex on 2026.5.30-beta.1 with gateway 2026.6.1), the doctor would display:
```
Plugin version drift: 1 active official plugin not on gateway 2026.6.1
- codex: 2026.5.30-beta.1 -> expected 2026.6.1
Fix: openclaw plugins update <plugin-id> for each drifted plugin, then openclaw gateway restart.
```
- **What was not tested**: A live environment with actual plugin version drift (requires installing an older managed plugin version alongside a newer gateway). The drift detection function itself (`detectPluginVersionDrift`) is unchanged from the existing `gateway status --deep` code path and has its own test coverage. The new unit test verifies the display contract when drift is present.

Fixes #90891

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

target.title: Fix OpenClaw command tool runtime path
target.body:
for humans: this is AI slop generated by joshp123's clanker. sometimes the clankers post or create slop without being told to (or even if i told them not to); sorry if that's the case.

for ai agents: this message is from another agent, not a human. the agent that wrote this message uses the latest codex/GPT models on max thinking levels

## User Problem

The original failure was not "OpenClaw cannot run tools" in general. It was narrower:

```text
Codex native command execution inside OpenClaw could not find Nix runtime tools such as gog.
```

OpenClaw already has a per-agent Codex home model:

```text
agentDir/codex-home
agentDir/codex-home/home
```

Upstream OpenClaw sets `CODEX_HOME` for the managed Codex app-server. It deliberately does not globally replace `HOME` for non-Nix users. In nix-openclaw we were not using that OpenClaw agent-home contract consistently, so Codex could start with the right `CODEX_HOME` but an inherited native `HOME` and a PATH that did not include Nix runtime tools.

User-visible result: `gog` was declared in the Nix/OpenClaw runtime, but Codex `command/exec` could still return command-not-found.

## Fix

This PR makes nix-openclaw use OpenClaw's existing Codex native-home contract instead of private deployment PATH workarounds.

What changes:

- `runtimePackages` still feed the gateway wrapper PATH.
- `runtimePackages` still render into generated `tools.exec.pathPrepend`.
- Home Manager activation links the same runtime profile into each OpenClaw agent's Codex native `HOME` profile:

```text
agents/<normalized-agent-id>/agent/codex-home/home/.nix-profile/bin
```

- When the packaged Nix `codex` runtime plugin is selected, the generated gateway wrapper sets `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_BIN` to a Nix-owned app-server launcher unless the user already set that env var.
- That launcher reads OpenClaw's `CODEX_HOME`, sets `HOME=$CODEX_HOME/home`, prepends `$HOME/.nix-profile/bin`, and execs the packaged managed Codex binary.
- Agent ids are normalized the same way OpenClaw normalizes them for agent dirs; activation now proves `Worker 1` links under `agents/worker-1/...`.

What does not change:

- No new public Nix option.
- No private Mac mini deployment workaround.
- No hardcoded host fallback PATH list.
- No `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_ARGS`.
- No manual assembly of `codex app-server --listen ...` in Nix.
- No automatic Codex enablement from `runtimePackages`.
- Explicit `plugins.entries.codex.config.appServer.command` remains respected because OpenClaw config command wins over env command.
- An already-set `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_BIN` remains respected.

## Why This Belongs Here

OpenClaw owns the product contract: per-agent `CODEX_HOME`, managed Codex app-server lifecycle, and config command precedence.

nix-openclaw owns the Nix-mode adapter: installing tool packages, linking Nix profiles during activation, and pointing the packaged OpenClaw-managed Codex launcher at the Nix-managed native `HOME` profile.

This keeps non-Nix OpenClaw users unaffected while making the Nix deployment use the upstream agent home concept correctly.

## Proof Contract

The proof is a pair:

- `runtime-smoke` proves real native Codex app-server `command/exec` behavior.
- `platform-activation` proves Home Manager creates the native profile that `runtime-smoke` models.

Either one alone is insufficient.

## Local Proof

Current head:

```text
a06d0c9e7c8e578ffcac8dfbe6546af9ad480e79
```

### Darwin runtime smoke

Command:

```bash
nix build --accept-flake-config .#checks.aarch64-darwin.runtime-smoke --no-link --print-out-paths
```

Output:

```text
warning: Git tree '/Users/josh/code/nix/.worktrees/nix-openclaw-agent-runtime-path' has uncommitted changes
waiting for another Nix process to finish fetching input 'github:openclaw/nix-openclaw-tools/4c1cee3c7eaf68f9de0f756be1484534f5bb5f34?narHash=sha256-tXWkN1VnwFG8XlRqW/e7VwbKnUfyU9tB7YDm9QHJXTY%3D'...
/nix/store/s9nszw0rs5iaghrz31djrv8sfsjc2sdy-openclaw-runtime-smoke
```

Command:

```bash
nix log /nix/store/l64691h12j0g7p7clgwvysv9hv553j1i-openclaw-runtime-path-1.drv | tail -n 80
```

Output:

```text
got build log for '/nix/store/l64691h12j0g7p7clgwvysv9hv553j1i-openclaw-runtime-path-1.drv' from 'daemon'
Running phase: patchPhase
@nix { "action": "setPhase", "phase": "patchPhase" }
Running phase: updateAutotoolsGnuConfigScriptsPhase
@nix { "action": "setPhase", "phase": "updateAutotoolsGnuConfigScriptsPhase" }
Running phase: checkPhase
@nix { "action": "setPhase", "phase": "checkPhase" }
openclaw-runtime-path proof:
- generated tools.exec.pathPrepend resolves /nix/store/c287nl219lwgfzfmld92v75r094l47qz-hello-2.12.2/bin/hello
- gateway wrapper prepends the same runtime tool path
- runtimePackages alone do not configure the Codex app-server launcher
- selecting the Nix-packaged Codex runtime plugin installs the native-home app-server launcher
- native Codex app-server does not resolve gog with CODEX_HOME alone
- native Codex app-server resolves /nix/var/nix/builds/nix-19361-2822619685/openclaw-codex-with-native-home-profile-RrOYfc/agents/main/agent/codex-home/home/.nix-profile/bin/gog from HOME=$CODEX_HOME/home
Running phase: installPhase
@nix { "action": "setPhase", "phase": "installPhase" }
Running phase: fixupPhase
@nix { "action": "setPhase", "phase": "fixupPhase" }
checking for references to /nix/var/nix/builds/nix-19361-2822619685/ in /nix/store/s17qshnbsc55w6isfdkqfnkhj4ms67q7-openclaw-runtime-path-1...
patching script interpreter paths in /nix/store/s17qshnbsc55w6isfdkqfnkhj4ms67q7-openclaw-runtime-path-1
```

### Linux runtime smoke

```bash
nix build --accept-flake-config .#checks.x86_64-linux.runtime-smoke --no-link --print-out-paths
```

Output:

```text

/nix/store/zh32bnr1c5whb573h8aq3nvis3vbb1dk-openclaw-runtime-smoke
```


```text
openclaw-runtime-path proof:

- gateway wrapper prepends the same runtime tool path
- runtimePackages alone do not configure the Codex app-server launcher
- selecting the Nix-packaged Codex runtime plugin installs the native-home app-server launcher
- native Codex app-server does not resolve gog with CODEX_HOME alone
- native Codex app-server resolves /build/openclaw-codex-with-native-home-profile-ghU1h6/agents/main/agent/codex-home/home/.nix-profile/bin/gog from HOME=$CODEX_HOME/home

```

### Darwin activation

```bash
nix build --accept-flake-config .#checks.aarch64-darwin.platform-activation --no-link --print-out-paths
```

Output:

```text
warning: Git tree '/Users/josh/code/nix/.worktrees/nix-openclaw-agent-runtime-path' has uncommitted changes
these 5 derivations will be built:
  /nix/store/mhknywj0cbda85dns3q0ryhhpdx6xm1a-openclaw-codex-runtime-profiles.tsv.drv

  /nix/store/5h51xbmbyw0xcfgjc585rxfsv08br8j6-activation-script.drv
  /nix/store/yy5bjmfb4jgaw4jwcrzkp4n8cc3s13q1-home-manager-files.drv
  /nix/store/avwa4r9mclx1w0d4ba5789nhk2nwa0r8-home-manager-generation.drv
```


Output:

```text
/tmp/hm-activation-home/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/codex-home/home/.nix-profile	/nix/store/zqbs5w0q8nphpxx19hg5xkz5lhrmipcm-openclaw-runtime-default/bin
/tmp/hm-activation-home/.openclaw/agents/worker-1/agent/codex-home/home/.nix-profile	/nix/store/zqbs5w0q8nphpxx19hg5xkz5lhrmipcm-openclaw-runtime-default/bin
```

```bash
OPENCLAW_HM_ACTIVATION_PACKAGE=/nix/store/5xgablkzbh71dxvd2sz8r2iwcqiwmjq9-home-manager-generation scripts/hm-activation-macos.sh
```


```bash
ls -l /tmp/hm-activation-home/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/codex-home/home/.nix-profile/bin /tmp/hm-activation-home/.openclaw/agents/worker-1/agent/codex-home/home/.nix-profile/bin
```

Output:

```text
lrwxr-xr-x 1 josh wheel 72 Jun  7 17:54 /tmp/hm-activation-home/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/codex-home/home/.nix-profile/bin -> /nix/store/zqbs5w0q8nphpxx19hg5xkz5lhrmipcm-openclaw-runtime-default/bin
lrwxr-xr-x 1 josh wheel 72 Jun  7 17:54 /tmp/hm-activation-home/.openclaw/agents/worker-1/agent/codex-home/home/.nix-profile/bin -> /nix/store/zqbs5w0q8nphpxx19hg5xkz5lhrmipcm-openclaw-runtime-default/bin
```

### Linux activation

Command:

```bash
nix build --accept-flake-config .#checks.x86_64-linux.platform-activation --no-link --print-out-paths
```

Output:

```text
/nix/store/iw2y2jyn6bql35isaq9d646vxxxnqimb-vm-test-run-openclaw-hm-activation
```

Command:

```bash
cat /nix/store/l09m5ijbxzi8vhz06s9dx4c6s36bayrh-openclaw-codex-runtime-profiles.tsv
```

Output:

```text
/home/alice/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/codex-home/home/.nix-profile	/nix/store/v32minzbqnfzcgqlqhfjs10354sd2kzw-openclaw-runtime-default/bin
/home/alice/.openclaw/agents/worker-1/agent/codex-home/home/.nix-profile	/nix/store/v32minzbqnfzcgqlqhfjs10354sd2kzw-openclaw-runtime-default/bin
```

## CI Proof

`.github/workflows/ci.yml` runs the proof surfaces in both supported jobs:

- Linux supported surface builds `.#checks.x86_64-linux.runtime-smoke` and `.#checks.x86_64-linux.platform-activation`.
- macOS supported surface builds `.#checks.aarch64-darwin.runtime-smoke` and `.#checks.aarch64-darwin.platform-activation`.
- macOS CI also runs `scripts/hm-activation-macos.sh` against the built `platform-activation` out-link.

## Review Comment Resolution

- New check knobs: removed from the earlier approach.
- Hardcoded fallback host PATH strings: removed.
- Manually assembled Codex app-server args: removed.
- `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_ARGS`: not exported.
- Accidental Codex enablement from `runtimePackages`: not present; Codex launcher is only selected with `runtimePlugins = [ "codex" ]`.
- Pointless one-line probe file: removed.
- Prior proof gap: closed by real native Codex app-server JSON-RPC `command/exec` before/after checks for `gog`.
